
---
title: "wbc population"
output: html_document
date: "2025-11-13"
---
---
title: "Untitled"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
date: "2025-11-10"
---
```{r, echo=FALSE}
 library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
Blood_count <- read_excel("smears_analysis_r.xlsx")
```



```{r, echo=FALSE}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    `total_cell` = `[Cells] c/ml`
  )

```


```{r, echo=FALSE}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    `Lympho%` = `Lymoph%`
  )

```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    `Shark_Id` = `Sharks Id`
  )
```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    `LG_ratio` = `L:G`
  )
```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    total_count        = `total count`
  )
```


```{r, echo=FALSE}
library(dplyr) # Make sure dplyr is loaded

# Fix: Explicitly pass the data frame as the first argument to rename()
Blood_count <- dplyr::rename(Blood_count, RBC_WBC = `rbc:wbc`)
```
```{r, echo=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# --- 1. CLEAN AND PREPARE DATA USING BASE R ---
# Force 'week' and 'Groupe' to be simple, clean vectors in the Blood_count frame.
# This addresses the "unique() applies only to vectors" error by breaking any nested structure.

# Convert necessary columns to character/numeric/factor explicitly
Blood_count$RBC_WBC_clean <- as.numeric(as.character(Blood_count$RBC_WBC))
Blood_count$week_clean <- as.numeric(as.character(Blood_count$week))
Blood_count$Groupe_clean <- factor(as.character(Blood_count$Groupe))

# Remove NA values for summarization
Blood_count_no_na <- na.omit(Blood_count[, c("week_clean", "Groupe_clean", "RBC_WBC_clean")])

# --- 2. SUMMARISE DATA (Using aggregate, a base R function) ---
# Calculate the mean and standard deviation (sd) for plotting
summary_data_rbcwbc <- aggregate(RBC_WBC_clean ~ week_clean + Groupe_clean, 
                          data = Blood_count_no_na, 
                          FUN = function(x) c(mean = mean(x), sd = sd(x)))

# Flatten the aggregated output into a usable data frame for ggplot
summary_data_rbcwbc <- data.frame(
  week = summary_data_rbcwbc$week_clean,
  Groupe = summary_data_rbcwbc$Groupe_clean,
  mean_RBC_WBC = summary_data_rbcwbc$RBC_WBC_clean[, "mean"],
  sd_RBC_WBC = summary_data_rbcwbc$RBC_WBC_clean[, "sd"]
)

# --- 3. CREATE PLOT ---
# Use the cleaned columns directly for plotting
grouped_barplot_rbcwbc <- ggplot(summary_data_rbcwbc, aes(x = factor(week), y = mean_RBC_WBC, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), color = "black") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_RBC_WBC - sd_RBC_WBC, ymax = mean_RBC_WBC + sd_RBC_WBC),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.2, color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "RBC:WBC ± SD",
    x = "Week",
    y = "Mean RBC:WBC",
    fill = "Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12)
  )

print(grouped_barplot_rbcwbc)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(car)        # For Levene's Test
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)   # For p-values in mixed models
library(MuMIn)      # For R²
library(DHARMa)     # For model diagnostics

# Make sure your ratio column is numeric and factors are correctly set
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(
    RBC_WBC = as.numeric(RBC_WBC),     # rename if your column is `rbc:wbc`
    week_factor = factor(week),
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id) # replace with your shark ID column name
  )

# ----- 📊 1. Levene’s Test (homogeneity of variance) -----
leveneTest(RBC_WBC ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor, data = Blood_count)

# ----- 🧪 2. Shapiro–Wilk Test (normality) -----
# Test normality of the raw RBC:WBC values
shapiro.test(Blood_count$RBC_WBC)

# (Optional visualization)
# ggplot(Blood_count, aes(sample = RBC_WBC)) + stat_qq() + stat_qq_line()

# ----- 📈 3. Fit GLMM -----
glmm_rbcwbc <- lmer(
  RBC_WBC ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
  data = Blood_count
)

# ----- 📄 4. Model Summary + R² -----
summary(glmm_rbcwbc)
r.squaredGLMM(glmm_rbcwbc)

# ----- 🧩 5. Residual diagnostics -----
simulation_output <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = glmm_rbcwbc, n = 250)
plot(simulation_output)

```
```{r}
anova_rbcwbc <- aov(RBC_WBC ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor, data = Blood_count)

# View the ANOVA table
summary(anova_rbcwbc)

# Optional: post-hoc pairwise comparisons if needed
TukeyHSD(anova_rbcwbc)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

# Pivot % columns into long format
Blood_long_pct <- Blood_count %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = matches("%$"),
    names_to = "Cell_var",
    values_to = "Percent"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    week = factor(week, levels = sort(unique(week))),
    Groupe = as.factor(Groupe)
  )

# Make a complete grid for all combinations
Blood_summary <- Blood_long_pct %>%
  group_by(Groupe, week, Cell_var) %>%
  summarise(mean_value = mean(Percent, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  complete(Groupe, week, Cell_var)

# Clean line plot
ggplot(Blood_summary, aes(x = week, y = mean_value, color = Groupe, group = Groupe)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~Cell_var, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  labs(x = "Week", y = "Mean (%)", color = "Group") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

```









```{r}
library(car)
# Make sure Groupe and week are factors
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(
    Groupe = factor(Groupe),
    week = factor(week)
  )
# [Rbc]
leveneTest(`[Rbc]` ~ Groupe * week, data = Blood_count)
shapiro.test(na.omit(Blood_count$`[Rbc]`))

# [wbc]
leveneTest(`[wbc]` ~ Groupe * week, data = Blood_count)
shapiro.test(na.omit(Blood_count$`[wbc]`))

# [LG_ratio]
leveneTest(`LG_ratio` ~ Groupe * week, data = Blood_count)
shapiro.test(na.omit(Blood_count$`LG_ratio`))

# [LG_ratio_gt]
leveneTest(`LG_ratio_gt` ~ Groupe * week, data = Blood_count)
shapiro.test(na.omit(Blood_count$`LG_ratio_gt`))

# [Granulocyte]
leveneTest(`[Granulocyte]` ~ Groupe * week, data = Blood_count)
shapiro.test(na.omit(Blood_count$`[Granulocyte]`))

# [Thrombo]
leveneTest(`[Thrombo]` ~ Groupe * week, data = Blood_count)
shapiro.test(na.omit(Blood_count$`[Thrombo]`))

# [Lympho]
leveneTest(`[Lympho]` ~ Groupe * week, data = Blood_count)
shapiro.test(na.omit(Blood_count$`[Lympho]`))

# [GT]
leveneTest(`[GT]` ~ Groupe * week, data = Blood_count)
shapiro.test(na.omit(Blood_count$`[GT]`))

```


```{r, warning=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Make sure factors are set
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(
    Groupe = factor(Groupe),
    week = factor(week)
  )

# Pivot all the relevant columns into long format
Blood_long <- Blood_count %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c( `[Thrombo]`, `[Lympho]`, `[GT]`, `[CE]` , `[FE]`),
    names_to = "Cell_var",
    values_to = "Value"
  )

# Faceted boxplot with custom fill colors
# Faceted boxplot with custom fill colors
ggplot(Blood_long, aes(x = week, y = Value, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55E00", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  labs(x = "Week", y = "Value", fill = "Group") +
  facet_wrap(~ Cell_var, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
     theme(
  strip.background = element_rect(
    fill = "#E0E0E0",  # soft grey
    color = "black",   # border color
    size = 0.3,        # border thickness
    linetype = "solid"
  ),
  strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
  
)
)

```
```{r, warning=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Make sure factors are set
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(
    Groupe = factor(Groupe),
    week = factor(week)
  )

# Pivot all the relevant columns into long format
Blood_long <- Blood_count %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(`[Rbc]`, `[wbc]`),
    names_to = "Cell_var",
    values_to = "Value"
  )

# Faceted boxplot with custom fill colors
ggplot(Blood_long, aes(x = week, y = Value, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55E00", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  labs(x = "Week", y = "Value", fill = "Group") +
  facet_wrap(~ Cell_var, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
     theme(
  strip.background = element_rect(
    fill = "#E0E0E0",  # soft grey
    color = "black",   # border color
    size = 0.3,        # border thickness
    linetype = "solid"
  ),
  strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
  
)
)
```



```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Make sure factors are set
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(
    Groupe = factor(Groupe),
    week = factor(week)
  )

# ---------------------------
# 1️⃣ [Rbc]
ggplot(Blood_count, aes(x = week, y = `[Rbc]`, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "[Rbc] by Week and Group", x = "Week", y = "[Rbc]") +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  theme_minimal()

# 2️⃣ [wbc]
ggplot(Blood_count, aes(x = week, y = `[wbc]`, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "[wbc] by Week and Group", x = "Week", y = "[wbc]") +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  theme_minimal()

# 3️⃣ LG_ratio (no brackets)
ggplot(Blood_count, aes(x = week, y = LG_ratio, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "LG_ratio by Week and Group", x = "Week", y = "LG_ratio") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  theme_minimal()

# 5️⃣ [Granulocyte]
ggplot(Blood_count, aes(x = week, y = `[Granulocyte]`, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "[Granulocyte] by Week and Group", x = "Week", y = "[Granulocyte]") +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  theme_minimal()

# 6️⃣ [Thrombo]
ggplot(Blood_count, aes(x = week, y = `[Thrombo]`, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "[Thrombo] by Week and Group", x = "Week", y = "[Thrombo]") +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  theme_minimal()

# 7️⃣ [Lympho]
ggplot(Blood_count, aes(x = week, y = `[Lympho]`, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "[Lympho] by Week and Group", x = "Week", y = "[Lympho]") +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  theme_minimal()

# 8️⃣ [GT]
ggplot(Blood_count, aes(x = week, y = `[GT]`, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "[GT] by Week and Group", x = "Week", y = "[GT]") +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Make sure factors are set
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(
    Groupe = factor(Groupe),
    week = factor(week)
  )

# ---------------------------
# 1️⃣ Rbc%
ggplot(Blood_count, aes(x = week, y = `Rbc%`, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Rbc% by Week and Group", x = "Week", y = "Rbc%") +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  theme_minimal()

#lympho
ggplot(Blood_count, aes(x = week, y = `Lympho%`, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Lympho% by Week and Group", x = "Week", y = "Rbc%") +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  theme_minimal()

# 2️⃣ wbc%
ggplot(Blood_count, aes(x = week, y = `wbc%`, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "wbc% by Week and Group", x = "Week", y = "wbc%") +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  theme_minimal()

# 3️⃣ thrombo%
ggplot(Blood_count, aes(x = week, y = `Thrombo%`, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "thrombo% by Week and Group", x = "Week", y = "thrombo%") +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  theme_minimal()

# 4️⃣ GT%
ggplot(Blood_count, aes(x = week, y = `GT%`, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "GT% by Week and Group", x = "Week", y = "GT%") +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  theme_minimal()

# 5️⃣ CE%
ggplot(Blood_count, aes(x = week, y = `CE%`, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "CE% by Week and Group", x = "Week", y = "CE%") +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  theme_minimal()

# 6️⃣ FE%
ggplot(Blood_count, aes(x = week, y = `FE%`, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "FE% by Week and Group", x = "Week", y = "FE%") +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  theme_minimal()

# 7️⃣ Neutro%
ggplot(Blood_count, aes(x = week, y = `Neutro%`, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Neutro% by Week and Group", x = "Week", y = "Neutro%") +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  theme_minimal()

# 8️⃣ Monocyte%
ggplot(Blood_count, aes(x = week, y = `Monocyte%`, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Monocyte% by Week and Group", x = "Week", y = "Monocyte%") +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  theme_minimal()

# 9️⃣ mitosis%
ggplot(Blood_count, aes(x = week, y = `mitosis%`, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "mitosis% by Week and Group", x = "Week", y = "mitosis%") +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  theme_minimal()

# 10️⃣ immature%
ggplot(Blood_count, aes(x = week, y = `immature%`, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "immature% by Week and Group", x = "Week", y = "immature%") +
   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73" )) +
  theme_minimal()

```


```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Filter for week 4
Blood_week4 <- Blood_count %>%
  filter(week == 4) %>%
  select(Groupe, `Lympho%`, `Thrombo%`, `GT%`, `FE%`, `CE%`, `Neutro%`, `Monocyte%`, `Basophil%`)

# Pivot longer
Blood_long_week4 <- Blood_week4 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(`Lympho%`, `Thrombo%`, `GT%`, `FE%`, `CE%`, `Neutro%`, `Monocyte%`, `Basophil%`),
    names_to = "Cell_type",
    values_to = "Percentage"
  ) %>%
  # Set the order of cell types
  mutate(Cell_type = factor(Cell_type, levels = c(
    "Lympho%", "Thrombo%", "GT%", "FE%", "CE%", "Neutro%", "Monocyte%", "Basophil%"
  )))

# Bar plot with dodged bars per cell type
ggplot(Blood_long_week4, aes(x = Cell_type, y = Percentage, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73")) +
  labs(title = "Week 4: Blood Cell Percentages by Group",
       x = "Cell Type", y = "Percentage", fill = "Group") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))


```
```{r}
# Filter for week 6
Blood_week6 <- Blood_count %>%
  filter(week == 6) %>%
  select(Groupe, `Lympho%`, `Thrombo%`, `GT%`, `FE%`, `CE%`, `Neutro%`, `Monocyte%`, `Basophil%`)

# Pivot longer and set order
Blood_long_week6 <- Blood_week6 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(`Lympho%`, `Thrombo%`, `GT%`, `FE%`, `CE%`, `Neutro%`, `Monocyte%`, `Basophil%`),
    names_to = "Cell_type",
    values_to = "Percentage"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Cell_type = factor(Cell_type, levels = c(
    "Lympho%", "Thrombo%", "GT%", "FE%", "CE%", "Neutro%", "Monocyte%", "Basophil%"
  )))

# Plot
ggplot(Blood_long_week6, aes(x = Cell_type, y = Percentage, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73")) +
  labs(title = "Week 6: Blood Cell Percentages by Group",
       x = "Cell Type", y = "Percentage", fill = "Group") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

```
```{r}

```

```{r}
# Filter for week 8
Blood_week8 <- Blood_count %>%
  filter(week == 8) %>%
  select(Groupe, `Lympho%`, `Thrombo%`, `GT%`, `FE%`, `CE%`, `Neutro%`, `Monocyte%`, `Basophil%`)

# Pivot longer and set order
Blood_long_week8 <- Blood_week8 %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(`Lympho%`, `Thrombo%`, `GT%`, `FE%`, `CE%`, `Neutro%`, `Monocyte%`, `Basophil%`),
    names_to = "Cell_type",
    values_to = "Percentage"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Cell_type = factor(Cell_type, levels = c(
    "Lympho%", "Thrombo%", "GT%", "FE%", "CE%", "Neutro%", "Monocyte%", "Basophil%"
  )))

# Plot
ggplot(Blood_long_week8, aes(x = Cell_type, y = Percentage, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Summer" = "#D55", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Control" = "#009E73")) +
  labs(title = "Week 8: Blood Cell Percentages by Group",
       x = "Cell Type", y = "Percentage", fill = "Group") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

```



```{r}
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)  # for p-values
library(DHARMa)    # for residual diagnostics
library(tidyverse)

# Make sure factors are correct
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe),
    week_factor = factor(week),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id)
  )

# -----------------------------
# 1️⃣ GLMM for absolute WBC counts
glmm_wbc <- lmer(`[wbc]` ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor+ (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
                 data = Blood_count)

summary(glmm_wbc)

# Check residuals
sim_wbc <- simulateResiduals(glmm_wbc, n = 250)
plot(sim_wbc)
testDispersion(sim_wbc)
testQuantiles(sim_wbc)
# Optional: testZeroInflation(sim_wbc)

# Optional manual residual plots
plot(fitted(glmm_wbc), resid(glmm_wbc))
abline(h = 0, col = "red")
qqnorm(resid(glmm_wbc)); qqline(resid(glmm_wbc))

# -----------------------------
# 2️⃣ GLMM for WBC percentages
glmm_wbc_percent <- lmer(`wbc%` ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
                         data = Blood_count)

summary(glmm_wbc_percent)

# Check residuals
sim_wbc_percent <- simulateResiduals(glmm_wbc_percent, n = 250)
plot(sim_wbc_percent)
testDispersion(sim_wbc_percent)
testQuantiles(sim_wbc_percent)
# Optional: testZeroInflation(sim_wbc_percent)

# Optional manual residual plots
plot(fitted(glmm_wbc_percent), resid(glmm_wbc_percent))
abline(h = 0, col = "red")
qqnorm(resid(glmm_wbc_percent)); qqline(resid(glmm_wbc_percent))
```
```{r}
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)  # for p-values
library(DHARMa)
library(tidyverse)

# Make sure factors are correct
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe),
    week_factor = factor(week),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id)
  )

# -----------------------------
# 1️⃣ GLMM for absolute Granulocyte counts
glmm_granulo <- lmer(`[Granulocyte]` ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
                     data = Blood_count)

summary(glmm_granulo)

# Check residuals with DHARMa
sim_granulo <- simulateResiduals(glmm_granulo, n = 250)
plot(sim_granulo)
testDispersion(sim_granulo)      # Over-/under-dispersion
testZeroInflation(sim_granulo)   # Check for excess zeros
testQuantiles(sim_granulo)       # Check quantile deviations

# Optional manual residual plots
plot(fitted(glmm_granulo), resid(glmm_granulo))
abline(h=0, col="red")
qqnorm(resid(glmm_granulo)); qqline(resid(glmm_granulo))

# -----------------------------
# 2️⃣ GLMM for Granulocyte percentages
glmm_granulo_percent <- lmer(`granulocyte%` ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
                             data = Blood_count)

summary(glmm_granulo_percent)

# Check residuals with DHARMa
sim_granulo_percent <- simulateResiduals(glmm_granulo_percent, n = 250)
plot(sim_granulo_percent)
testDispersion(sim_granulo_percent)
testQuantiles(sim_granulo_percent)
# Percentages typically won't have zero-inflation, but can test if needed
# testZeroInflation(sim_granulo_percent)

# Optional manual residual plots
plot(fitted(glmm_granulo_percent), resid(glmm_granulo_percent))
abline(h=0, col="red")
qqnorm(resid(glmm_granulo_percent)); qqline(resid(glmm_granulo_percent))

```
```{r}
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest) 
library(tidyverse)


# -----------------------------
# 1️⃣ LMM for absolute Lymphocyte counts: `[Lympho]`
# Note: Excluding Sex, mirroring your [wbc] and [Granulocyte] models.
lmm_lympho <- lmer(`[Lympho]` ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
data = Blood_count)

summary(lmm_lympho)

# Check residuals with DHARMa
sim_lympho <- simulateResiduals(lmm_lympho, n = 250)
plot(sim_lympho)

# DHARMa Tests
cat("\n--- DHARMa Tests for [Lympho] ---\n")
testDispersion(sim_lympho) 
testZeroInflation(sim_lympho) 
testQuantiles(sim_lympho) # Check quantile deviations (most important test)


# -----------------------------
# 2️⃣ LMM for Lymphocyte percentages: `lympho%`
# Note: Including Sex_factor (assuming that is the correct column name for Sex in your data).
lmm_lympho_percent <- lmer(`Lympho%` ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
data = Blood_count)

summary(lmm_lympho_percent)

# Check residuals with DHARMa
sim_lympho_percent <- simulateResiduals(lmm_lympho_percent, n = 250)
plot(sim_lympho_percent)

# DHARMa Tests
cat("\n--- DHARMa Tests for lympho% ---\n")
testDispersion(sim_lympho_percent)
testQuantiles(sim_lympho_percent)
```

```{r, error=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# List of columns to plot
cell_cols <- c("[Rbc]", "[wbc]", "[Lympho]", "[Thrombo]", "[GT]", "[CE]", "[FE]", "[Granulocyte]")

# Loop to create plots for each column
for (col in cell_cols) {
  
  # Wrap column name in backticks for aes_string
  col_aes <- paste0("`", col, "`")
  
  p <- Blood_count %>%
    ggplot(aes_string(x = "factor(week)", y = col_aes, fill = "Groupe")) +
    geom_bar(stat = "summary", fun = "mean", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), color = "black") +
     scale_fill_manual(values = c("Control" = "#009E73", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Summer" = "#D55")) +
    geom_errorbar(stat = "summary", fun.data = mean_se, 
                  position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.2, color = "red") +
    labs(
      title = paste0(col, " concentration ± SE"),
      x = "Week",
      y = paste0("Mean ", col),
      fill = "Group"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 12)
    )
  
  print(p)
}


```
```{r, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

percent_cols <- c("Rbc%", "wbc%", "granulocyte%", "Lympho%", "Thrombo%", "GT%", "CE%", "FE%")

for (col in percent_cols) {
  
  p <- Blood_count %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = factor(week), y = .data[[col]], fill = Groupe)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "summary", fun = mean, 
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), color = "black") +
     scale_fill_manual(values = c("Control" = "#009E73", "Winter" = "#56B4E9", "Summer" = "#D55")) +
    geom_errorbar(stat = "summary", fun.data = mean_se, 
                  position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.2, color = "red") +
    labs(
      title = paste0(col, " (%) ± SE"),
      x = "Week",
      y = paste0("Mean ", col),
      fill = "Group"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 12)
    )
  
  print(p)
}

```



```{r}
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest) 
library(DHARMa)

# Note: Ensure the RBC_WBC column exists in Blood_count

# --- 1. LMM for RBC_WBC Ratio ---
lmm_RBC_WBC_ratio <- lmer(RBC_WBC ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex +  (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
                         data = Blood_count)

summary(lmm_RBC_WBC_ratio)

# --- 2. Check residuals with DHARMa ---
sim_RBC_WBC <- simulateResiduals(lmm_RBC_WBC_ratio, n = 250)

print("\n--- DHARMa Residual Plots and Tests for RBC_WBC Ratio ---")
plot(sim_RBC_WBC)

# DHARMa Tests
testDispersion(sim_RBC_WBC)       # Over-/under-dispersion
testQuantiles(sim_RBC_WBC)        # Check quantile deviations


```
